topic: Centennial Hills Handyman: High-Ticket Repair Packages for Bigger Homes, Exterior Wear, and Whole-House Punch Lists
excerpt: Centennial Hills is positioned as a spacious, family-friendly northwest Las Vegas community with newer housing and room to spread out, and that changes the repair pattern a bit. Instead of one tiny isolated problem, we usually see whole-house punch-list work: upstairs drywall seams showing in hallway light, exterior trim opening on the hot side, garage-to-house doors drifting, and gates getting fussy after a windy stretch. Underneath, the valley’s native soils are shallow, well-drained alluvial soils with carbonates/caliche, so small movement can still telegraph as latch misalignment, recurring cracks, and fence drift even in newer homes. Then the climate does what Vegas always does—extreme heat, strong sun, and monsoon-season flash flooding stress sealants, hardware, coatings, and drainage details all at once. Around Centennial Hills, the durable fix is usually a bundled high-ticket scope: tighten the openings, reinforce the finishes, correct the water path, and clean up the exterior details so the house feels maintained instead of patched in pieces.
markdown_tables:
| High-ticket Centennial Hills package | What it usually includes | Representative total |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Whole-house punch-list reset | doors, hardware, wall repair, trim, caulk, second-pass details | $2,500–$7,000 |
| Multi-room wall and ceiling finish package | recurring cracks, wide floats, texture blending, prime-ready cleanup | $2,000–$8,000 |
| Exterior trim and seal scope | sun-baked trim repairs, resealing, fascia/soffit touchpoints, paint-ready prep | $2,500–$9,000 |
| Fence and gate rebuild package | post/rail corrections, gate alignment, hardware reset | $2,000–$7,500 |
| Pre-listing “show-ready” package | high-visibility fixes inside and out, plus final punch pass | $3,500–$11,500 |
| Water-control and storm-prep bundle | gutter corners, discharge fixes, splash-back corrections, exterior reseals | $2,000–$8,500 |
